issues.title: resyncSchema refuse path leaves stray migration decls in migrations.json
issues.body: Found by the S4 adversarial audit (TASK-026 / FGAP-029) before commit. On a version-bump re-sync, resyncSchema appends the shipped migration declarations into the substrate's migrations.json before it forward-validates the block. When the migrated items would fail the new schema, validation throws and the outcome is 'blocked'; the catch block restores the schema file bytes and invalidates the in-process registry cache, but it does not undo the decls already written to migrations.json. A refused re-sync therefore leaves stray migration entries on disk, contradicting the function docstring's claim that a 'blocked' outcome leaves files byte-identical to their pre-call state. Latent in current project state (the idempotency guard prevents a collision crash on re-run, and no live re-sync currently reaches the failing-items path), but a real correctness bug in the refuse path. Planned fix: capture migrations.json raw bytes before the decl-append loop and restore them (or unlink if the file did not exist pre-call) in the existing catch, mirroring the originalSchemaBytes capture/restore already in the same function; add a migrations.json-byte-unchanged assertion to the two 'blocked' tests. Fixed and closed within TASK-026 (an S4 sub-defect, not a separate arc).
issues.resolved_by: 0fcba9c — resyncSchema captures migrations.json raw bytes before the decl-append loop and, on the blocked refuse path, restores them (or unlinks the file if it did not exist pre-call) in the catch, so a refused re-sync leaves migrations.json byte-unchanged; the two blocked-path tests in install-subcommand.test.ts assert migrations.json byte-identity after a blocked outcome.

issues.title: layer-plans schema nests id-bearing arrays (plans.layers, plans.migration_phases) instead of top-level entities + membership edges
issues.body: plans[].layers[] and plans[].migration_phases[] are nested id-bearing arrays; id-bearing items must be top-level entities joined by membership edges (edges-not-inline). context-validate flags both (code nested_id_bearing_array). Fix (schema-only; layer-plans has 0 items, no data migration): promote layers and migration_phases to top-level entity kinds, link each to its parent plan via a membership relation_type, drop the nested arrays from the layer-plans item schema; apply to the samples catalog and the live installed copy.

issues.title: update refused conventions items that by inspection satisfy the target schema
issues.body: A conventions 1.0.0->1.0.1 forward-migration blocked items that, by the operator's inspection, satisfy 1.0.1: every rule carries description, enforcement, and severity; all enforcement values are within {lint,test,review,manual} and all severity values within {error,warning,info}; the migration is identity. dry-run predicted resynced; the live forward-migrate + re-validate refused, leaving schema and block byte-unchanged. Root cause undetermined. The two capabilities confirmation was originally gated on have since shipped: the per-item schema-validation diagnostic (blockedDetail.failures naming item id/field/keyword, TASK-048/STORY-009) and dry/live outcome parity (simulateResyncOutcome, STORY-008). Residual: re-drive the operator-substrate reproduction (conventions installed at 1.0.0, 45 rules) with the diagnostic to name the failing item/field, then fix or retire this issue on that evidence. Not reproducible against this repo's .context, where conventions is in-sync at 1.0.1 and validates cleanly.
issues.resolved_by: issue-003 re-drive (analysis/2026-07-05-issue-003-redrive-pre-identity-refusal.md) — root cause determined by two-cell single-variable reproduction: the refusal is the mandatory identity stamp (writeBlockForDir's substrateIdForDir throw on a config with no substrate_id) caught by resyncSchema's catch and reported as blocked/validation-failed; the items were never invalid, and the identical fixture WITH a substrate_id migrates cleanly. Dry-run diverges because simulateResyncOutcome never exercises the write/stamp precondition. No validation defect exists; the mechanism is the already-filed pre-identity resync gap, and the diagnostic misattribution surfaced by the re-drive is filed as its own class-level gap.

issues.title: npm link couples the operator pi-context binary to the dev dist/ — a dev build mutates/bricks the live binary
issues.body: The global pi-context is an npm link symlink to packages/pi-context-cli/dist/bin.js; the package build is `rm -rf dist && tsc -p tsconfig.build.json && chmod +x dist/bin.js`. So npm run build repoints — and transiently (rm -rf) removes — the live-resolving operator binary: the dogfooding/operator binary IS the dev build target, with no separate installed copy. Incident: the TASK-020 config-schema bump rebuilt the linked binary to a new schema while live .context/config.json was still the old shape, so every config-loading op exit-5 bricked until the change was reverted. This is the binary-identity root coupling of that incident, distinct from the validation/migration side (FGAP-095 no load-time config migration / FGAP-096 validate-before-repair deadlock / FGAP-097 no additive discipline) and from FGAP-089 (PreToolUse hook scope). Structural fix (analysis/2026-06-18-proving-ground-prior-art-research.md): make the operator binary a COPY via npm pack + npm i -g <tarball> (or a custom prefix), refreshed by an explicit publish-free local promote step — symlink-free, so a routine dev build cannot touch it; npm link also does not validate packaging (files/exports/bin), so a packed-copy additionally catches packaging drift. Verified mechanics + derivation in analysis/2026-06-18-proving-ground-prior-art-research.md and analysis/2026-06-18-isolated-proving-ground-method.md.
issues.resolved_by: TASK-069 (scripts/promote-cli.mjs + the npm run promote:cli root task), final fix 409a71d; verified VER-055 against the real global /opt/homebrew. The npm-link coupling is retired: the live operator pi-context is now a packed COPY under /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@davidorex/pi-context-cli (a real directory, no symlink into the repo), installed via npm pack + npm i -g <tarball...> --force of the @davidorex workspace set. The root coupling is closed by construction: a repo npm run build (rm -rf dist && tsc) leaves the installed bin's inode + content hash unchanged (VER-055 criterion 4), and the operator runs with the repo dist deleted (criterion 6). issue-006 (poisonable/unvalidated-target destructive op) and issue-007 (validate-vs-act TOCTOU), surfaced while hardening the promote, are resolved alongside.

issues.title: update same-version resync verbatim-overwrites the installed schema with no block-item re-validation — a narrowing catalog change silently invalidates existing items
issues.body: update routes a catalog-ahead schema whose body changed at the SAME version through resyncSchema's verbatim-overwrite arm (index.ts:1066): the installed schema file is replaced with the catalog body and no existing block item is re-validated against it. A catalog change that narrows validity at the same version — dropping a property under additionalProperties:false, adding a required field, narrowing an enum — silently leaves existing block items invalid, with no refusal and no warning. This is asymmetric with the version-bump path, which forward-migrates + re-validates the items and refuses (blocked, byte-unchanged) when they would fail. Concretely: the catalog story schema omits user_kind under additionalProperties:false while 14 live story items carry user_kind; update --dryRun classifies that schema resync (not blocked), so applying it invalidates those items.

issues.title: promote-cli runs `npm rm -g` against a poisonable, unvalidated target prefix before any guard
issues.body: scripts/promote-cli.mjs resolves the install target via `npm prefix -g` (resolveTargetPrefix, line 107), which echoes an inherited `npm_config_prefix` rather than the true global; an invocation that set `--prefix` (e.g. `npm --prefix <dir> run`) exports `npm_config_prefix` into the child, so the resolved target becomes that value. `isRealGlobal` (line 116) is derived from the source label alone, which survives the poisoning, so a poisoned prefix is classified as the real global. When `isRealGlobal` is true the script runs the destructive `npm rm -g @davidorex/pi-context-cli` (line 153) before installing and before any validation of the target. The only validation — the realpath `startsWith(REPO_ROOT)` check (line 177) — is post-install, too late to prevent the rm. Effect: a global removal runs against an unvalidated prefix. Reproducing condition: `npm --prefix <repo> run promote:cli` resolves the target to the repo root, classifies it `isRealGlobal` true, and installs under the repo (`bin/`, `lib/node_modules/`); the post-install guard then aborts (exit 1). The destructive `npm rm -g` is aimed at the same poisoned prefix, so it does not touch the real global in this path — but the rm still runs unguarded against an unvalidated target. Class: a destructive operation executed before its precondition is validated, compounded by trusting a poisonable ambient environment for a safety-critical target. Fix: resolve the real global from a source that does not honor an inherited `npm_config_prefix` (or refuse the implicit real-global arm when the prefix is inherited); hoist a pre-destruction validation before line 153 carrying the `REPO_ROOT` check plus an intended-global assertion, so no destructive op runs until the target is validated; retain the post-install guard as defense-in-depth. Post-condition: no destructive op executes against an unvalidated target.
issues.resolved_by: 409a71d (iterate-5: removed the refuse-on-inherited block) atop 73ede54/57d331d/8d4bdc7/60613cc; verified VER-055 against the real global. issue-006's post-condition (no destructive op executes against an unvalidated target) is met: the sole destructive global op (npm i -g --force) is reachable only after assertSafeTargetPrefix returns the fully-resolved validated realpath; the separate npm rm -g was dropped (npm i -g --force replaces an existing link). The poisonable-target class is closed by the cleanNpmEnv() scrub on both the npm prefix -g probe and the install (an inherited npm_config_prefix=/tmp/POISON resolves the true global /opt/homebrew regardless), NOT by refusing inherited prefixes (that refusal, the F2 guard added in 57d331d, broke the documented npm run promote:cli path and was removed in 409a71d as a determinate defect).

issues.title: promote-cli validates the resolved realpath of the target prefix but passes the literal unresolved prefix to npm i -g --prefix
issues.body: assertSafeTargetPrefix validates the target prefix by realpath of its nearest-existing ancestor against the repo-root realpath; the install passes the literal unresolved prefix to npm i -g --prefix. The validated representation and the acted-upon representation differ. A symlinked path component of prefix re-pointed in the window between the validate call and the npm install — a window that spans the build and pack steps — is resolved by npm at install time but was not present at validation, so the global install can land on a target the validation never approved, including inside the repo. Precondition: a concurrent writer to a component of the resolved path during the run. Class: validated-target diverges from acted-upon-target — a TOCTOU between a realpath-validated check and a literal-path use.
issues.resolved_by: 60613cc (iterate-4: materialize + fully-resolve + re-validate the target prefix); verified VER-055 (criterion 11 passed) + the iterate-5 adversarial probe. The validated-target-diverges-from-acted-upon-target TOCTOU is closed: assertSafeTargetPrefix now mkdir -p materializes the target, realpathSync-resolves it fully, re-runs the repo-containment refusals on that resolved path, and RETURNS the fully-resolved realpath (safePrefix); the install (npm i -g --prefix safePrefix), the binPath verify, and the location logs all consume safePrefix, so no literal unresolved leaf and no symlink component survives to be re-pointed between validation and install. VER-054 had recorded the leaf-segment residual failed for a not-yet-created override target; 60613cc removed that residual.

issues.title: promote-cli resolveTargetPrefix detects --prefix only in space-separated form; the glued --prefix=<dir> falls through to the real-global arm
issues.body: resolveTargetPrefix detects the --prefix argument via argv.indexOf('--prefix'), matching only the space-separated form. npm's glued form --prefix=<dir> is a single argv token, so indexOf returns -1, resolution falls past the PROMOTE_PREFIX arm, and the run takes the real-global arm. A promote invoked as --prefix=/tmp/x targets the real global prefix (e.g. /opt/homebrew) instead of the intended throwaway target. The real-global arm is itself validated, so the safety invariant holds; the defect defeats the caller's chosen target. Class: an input form accepted in one spelling and mis-routed in another.
issues.resolved_by: 8d4bdc7 (iterate-3); verified VER-054 — glued --prefix=<dir> honored as override, installs at <dir>/bin/pi-context

issues.title: promote-cli post-install verify guard checks realpathSync(binPath).startsWith(REPO_ROOT) — literal REPO_ROOT, no path-separator boundary
issues.body: The post-install verify guard checks realpathSync(binPath).startsWith(REPO_ROOT): literal REPO_ROOT, no trailing-separator boundary. The pre-install validation checks against REPO_ROOT_REAL (realpath-normalized) with a trailing separator. Two divergent representations of the same repo-containment property yield two manifestations: (1) missing separator boundary — a sibling directory whose path shares the REPO_ROOT string as a prefix matches as contained; (2) missing realpath normalization — a REPO_ROOT that differs from its own realpath is not matched against the binPath's resolved form. The guard aborts on match (fail-safe direction): a consistency/correctness defect, not an exploit. Class: two guards asserting one repo-containment property over divergent path representations.
issues.resolved_by: 8d4bdc7 (iterate-3); verified VER-054 — post-install guard compares realpathSync(binPath) against REPO_ROOT_REAL + sep, matching validation

issues.title: Active .context is the pi-context dev substrate, not a clean baseline for the operation-framework port
issues.body: The WASC operation-framework port (PHASE-PORT-OPS) needs a substrate baseline. The active .context is the pi-context development substrate, carrying its own 74 tasks / 101 gaps / 18 decisions / 14 stories and accumulated suboptimal content; it is not a clean baseline. Building the port on it co-mingles the port's PM state with the pi-context dev arc and inherits the suboptimal content.

issues.title: workflows extension ctrl+j shortcut shadows pi built-in newline (tui.input.newLine); ctrl+h byte-aliases legacy Backspace — chords registered with no built-in-set check
issues.body: pi-workflows registers two editor-global shortcuts (packages/pi-workflows/src/index.ts:1034,1042; shipped via the meta-package's workflows-extension.ts re-export): ctrl+h = pause running workflow, ctrl+j = resume paused workflow. pi's built-in tui.input.newLine defaults to [shift+enter, ctrl+j] (pi-tui keybindings.js:66). pi's loader resolves non-reserved collisions extension-wins with a startup diagnostic (pi-coding-agent runner.js:309), and the editor checks extension shortcuts before all built-in handling (custom-editor.js:26), so with the extension loaded ctrl+j triggers workflow-resume instead of inserting a newline. On terminals without Kitty/modifyOtherKeys, shift+enter is indistinguishable from enter, making ctrl+j the only newline chord — keyboard newline entry is lost entirely there; an accidental ctrl+j can also silently resume a stale incomplete run. Sibling (latent, not loader-flagged — loader compares chord strings only): ctrl+h emits 0x08, which pi-tui matchesRawBackspace (keys.js:544) treats as plain Backspace on non-Windows-Terminal setups, so terminals whose Backspace sends raw 0x08 trigger the pause handler on Backspace. Class: both chords were authored with no check against pi's default binding set; pi affords no registration-time conflict query (registerShortcut returns void, types.d.ts:859). Resolution: rebind resume to alt+r and pause to alt+p (absent from all pi defaults, no control-byte alias; ctrl+q rejected: XON), sweep the advertised chord strings (workflow-executor.ts:627,839,966; step-pause.ts docstring; pausable-analysis.workflow.yaml:20; README/SKILL surfaces), and comment the registration site with the chord-selection constraint (defaults-free + no control-byte alias: ctrl+h=BS, ctrl+i=Tab, ctrl+j=LF, ctrl+m=CR). Note /workflow has a resume subcommand but no pause subcommand — ctrl+h is the only pause surface. Investigation: analysis/2026-07-06-workflows-extension-ctrlj-shortcut-conflict.md.

issues.title: pi-agent-dispatch loop/tool/type doc comments cite frozen .project-era IDs (FEAT-006/TASK-088/TASK-091/DEC-0044/DEC-0047/DEC-0018/DEC-0014) that collide with unrelated active-.context items of the same IDs
issues.body: The work-order loop and its tools carry provenance comments referencing IDs from the frozen .project-era substrate: run-work-order-loop-tool.ts:4 still says 'loads from .project/work-orders.json'; work-order-loop.ts and types.ts cite FEAT-006 'north-star loop', TASK-088, TASK-091, DEC-0044/0047/0018/0014 as dispatch decisions. In the active .context these IDs resolve to unrelated items — resolve-items-by-id: DEC-0044/DEC-0047 = null, FEAT-006 = the pi-context update feature, TASK-088 = write-time invariant evaluation. Anyone tracing the loop's provenance from the active substrate is misdirected. Surfaced by the 2026-07-07 work-order dogfood investigation (analysis/2026-07-07-work-order-dispatch-dogfood-breaks.md §cross-cutting). Resolution: when FEAT-014 touches these files, re-anchor the comments to active-substrate IDs or mark them explicitly as frozen-.project-era provenance; correct the stale '.project/work-orders.json' path string.
issues.resolved_by: TASK-108 (commit ea27ee25, merged to main): work-order-loop.ts and run-work-order-loop-tool.ts's frozen .project-era ID citations (FEAT-006/TASK-088/TASK-091/DEC-0044/DEC-0047/DEC-0018/DEC-0014) and the stale .project/work-orders.json path string are replaced with plain-English rationale, per the broader FGAP-136 standard (opacity to an outside reader, not staleness, is the actual defect). Correction to this issue's own filed body: it names 'types.ts' alongside work-order-loop.ts, but no types.ts file exists in pi-agent-dispatch/src -- the DEC-0047 citations matching that description are in packages/pi-jit-agents/src/types.ts, a different package, out of this fix's scope and tracked as FGAP-136 follow-on.
